original_abstract: The legal system of each country should provide for specific regulations in the event of a threat to the functioning, and even more so, the existence of the state. These situations are states of emergency. Responsibilities relating to the security of the state and its citizens rest primarily with state administration bodies. Legal provisions should be constructed in such a way that in emergency cases there are no doubts as to the scope of liability of each of them. A state of emergency can only be introduced when ordinary constitutional measures are insufficient. The purpose of this article is to present and discuss the development of the institution of states of emergency in reborn Poland based on the March Constitution of March 17, 1921 and the April Constitution of April 23, 1935, as well as other legal acts issued in the period in question and having a significant impact on impact on the security of the Polish state. In the period in question, the application of specific legal regulations was justified by the current internal or external situation of the country. The article uses dogmatic-legal and historical-legal methods.
humanized_abstract: The need for regulations in the event of threats to the functioning and in particular to the existence of the state is met in each country’s law through specific regulations on states of emergency. The obligation to secure the state and its citizens is largely borne by state administrative organs. Regulations concerning the event of an emergency should not give way to any interpretation as to the liability of these bodies in this respect. A state of emergency can only be introduced in the case of the failure of normal constitutional means to function. The purpose of this article is to reflect on and present the evolution of the state of emergency institution in the newly restored Polish state, based on the March Constitution of 17th of March 1921 and the April Constitution of 23rd of April 1935, as well as other acts that were adopted during this period and which, due to their importance, influenced the regulation of the state’s security. In the period under consideration, the adoption of special legal regulations for given situations was due to the current internal or external situation of the state. The article was written using the dogmatic-legal and historical-legal methods of research.
